title: Angular Basic — Paper 5
description: Foundational Angular concepts: components, data binding, directives, component communication, lifecycle hooks, services, modules, template-driven forms, pipes, and CLI.

questions.text: Which property in the @Component decorator specifies inline CSS styles directly within the decorator (as opposed to a file)?

options.text: styleUrls
cssText
styles
inlineStyles

questions.text: In Angular, what does [attr.aria-label]="labelText" demonstrate?

options.text: Event binding to an aria label event
Two-way binding of an ARIA attribute
Attribute binding used to set an HTML attribute that has no corresponding DOM property
Property binding to the aria label DOM property

questions.text: In the Angular 17+ control flow syntax, what is the equivalent of *ngIf with an else clause?

options.text: @if (condition) { ... } @otherwise { ... }
@if (condition) { ... } @else { ... }
@condition (condition) { ... } @fallback { ... }
@when (condition) { ... } @default { ... }

questions.text: Which Angular decorator queries all instances of a directive or component in the projected content (not the view)?

options.text: @ViewChildren
@ContentChildren
@QueryList
@ProjectedChildren

questions.text: What is the ngAfterContentChecked lifecycle hook used for?

options.text: It is called once after the content is projected for the first time
It is called after every check of the component's projected content by Angular's change detection
It is called when the component's view is created
It is called when the content projection fails validation

questions.text: What is the hierarchical injector in Angular?

options.text: A list of services sorted by priority
An injector that can only inject one service at a time
A tree of injectors where child injectors can access parent injector services unless overridden locally
An injector that inherits services from sibling components

questions.text: In Angular, what is a feature module?

options.text: The main application module that bootstraps the app
A module that groups related components, directives, and pipes for a specific application feature
A module provided by the Angular framework itself
A module that only contains service providers

questions.text: In a template-driven form, when is a control considered 'invalid'?

options.text: When the user has not focused on it yet
When its value fails one or more of its configured validators
When the form has been submitted
When the input element is disabled

questions.text: How do you set the locale for the 'currency' pipe to display British pounds in Angular?

options.text: {{ price | currency:'GBP':'symbol':'1.2-2' }}
{{ price | currency.GBP }}
{{ price | currency:'British' }}
{{ price | money:'GBP' }}

questions.text: Which configuration file defines build targets, assets, styles, and scripts for an Angular project?

options.text: tsconfig.json
package.json
angular.json
karma.conf.js

questions.text: What does setting standalone: true in the @Component decorator indicate?

options.text: The component cannot be used inside another component
The component is self-contained and does not need to be declared in an NgModule
The component runs outside of the Angular zone
The component does not have a selector

questions.text: What is the result of using interpolation to bind a non-string value such as a boolean or object in the template?

options.text: Angular throws a compilation error
The value is automatically converted to its string representation via .toString()
Angular ignores the binding and leaves the text blank
The component crashes at runtime

questions.text: In Angular's @switch control flow block, what is the purpose of the @default block?

options.text: It is the first case evaluated in the switch
It sets the default value for the switch variable
It renders when no @case expression matches the switch value
It provides a loading state while cases are evaluated

questions.text: What is the purpose of the @Input() alias parameter, e.g. @Input('aliasName')?

options.text: It renames the property inside the component class
It allows the parent to bind using a different name than the component's internal property name
It makes the input property optional
It creates a computed property

questions.text: How should you unsubscribe from an Observable in the ngOnDestroy lifecycle hook to avoid memory leaks?

options.text: Call observable.close() in ngOnDestroy
Store the subscription and call subscription.unsubscribe() in ngOnDestroy
Angular automatically unsubscribes when the component is destroyed
Set the observable to null in ngOnDestroy

questions.text: Which approach allows you to provide a different implementation of a service for testing purposes in Angular?

options.text: Changing the @Injectable decorator
Overriding the service using the providers array with useClass or useValue in the test module
Editing the original service class
Setting a global override flag in angular.json

questions.text: What does the app.config.ts file configure in a standalone Angular application?

options.text: It sets up the application's CSS theme
It defines route guards and interceptors only
It configures application-level providers, such as routing and HTTP client, passed to bootstrapApplication()
It declares all standalone components used in the application

questions.text: In a template-driven form, how do you display an error message only when a specific field is both invalid and touched?

options.text: Using *ngIf="fieldRef.invalid" without any condition
Using *ngIf="fieldRef.invalid && fieldRef.touched"
Using *ngIf="form.submitted"
Using *ngIf="fieldRef.errors"

questions.text: Which of the following correctly demonstrates using the 'async' pipe in a template?

options.text: {{ myObservable.subscribe() | async }}
{{ await myObservable | display }}
{{ myObservable | async }}
{{ myObservable | subscribe }}

questions.text: What does the ng build --configuration=production flag do differently from a default build?

options.text: It skips TypeScript compilation
It applies optimizations like minification, tree-shaking, and AOT compilation using production environment settings
It generates only HTML and CSS files
It runs the application without a web server

questions.text: Which of the following correctly creates a standalone component that imports Angular's CommonModule?

options.text: @Component({ standalone: true, declarations: [CommonModule], ... })
@Component({ standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule], ... })
@Component({ imports: [CommonModule], standalone: false, ... })
@NgModule({ imports: [CommonModule], standalone: true })

questions.text: Which style binding syntax would you use to conditionally set only the 'font-size' style of an element in Angular?

options.text: [class.font-size]="fontSize + 'px'"
[ngStyle]="{ fontSize: fontSize }"
[style.font-size]="fontSize + 'px'"
(style)="font-size: fontSize"

questions.text: In the Angular @for control flow block, which expression is mandatory for performance optimization (similar to trackBy)?

options.text: limit expression
sort expression
key expression
track expression

questions.text: What does the Output() alias allow you to do in a child component?

options.text: Bind the output to multiple parent components simultaneously
Allow the parent to listen using a different event name than the component's internal EventEmitter property name
Make the output emit synchronously instead of asynchronously
Prevent the event from bubbling up to grandparent components

questions.text: Which lifecycle hook is called after Angular has checked the component's view and its child views following the initial render?

options.text: ngAfterContentChecked
ngAfterViewChecked
ngDoCheck
ngOnInit

questions.text: What is tree-shaking in the context of Angular and services?

options.text: A process that removes duplicate DOM nodes from the template
A build optimization that removes unused code (including unused services) from the final bundle
A runtime optimization that reduces injector hierarchy depth
A way to remove circular dependencies between services

questions.text: In Angular, what is a shared module?

options.text: A module that can only be used once across the application
The root application module
A module containing reusable components, directives, and pipes that are exported for use in multiple feature modules
A module that shares state between components using a store

questions.text: What Angular CSS class is added to a template-driven form control when it fails validation?

options.text: ng-dirty
ng-invalid
ng-error
ng-untouched

questions.text: When should you prefer using the 'async' pipe over manually subscribing to an Observable in a component?

options.text: When you need to transform the Observable's data before displaying it
When working with HTTP requests that never complete
When you want Angular to automatically handle subscription and unsubscription, reducing the risk of memory leaks
Only when the Observable emits a single value

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'main.ts' file in an Angular standalone application?

options.text: It declares all standalone components used in the app
It defines all the application routes
It is the entry point that calls bootstrapApplication() to start the Angular application
It configures the TypeScript compiler options
